sections.paragraph:
ἔκπληξις, εως, ἡ (s. preceding entry; Hippoc., Aeschyl, Thu. et al.; 1 Km 14:15 Aq; Job 4:13 Sym.; Ps 30:23 al. Sym.; EpArist 96:99; Joseph.) state or condition of being astonished, amazement GJs 18:3 v.l. (s. Strycker 151, note).—S. DELG s.v. πλήσσω.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Strycker Strycker = ÉdeStrycker, s. GJs in List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 308
